findings.description: PermissionDialog component shows risk-level-colored dialogs (low=blue, medium=yellow, high=red) with tool-specific icons (Bash=Terminal, Write/Edit=Pencil, etc.) and three decision options: Allow Once, Allow Always, Deny
findings.evidence: PermissionDialog.tsx: riskBorderClass/riskBgClass maps for 3 risk levels, RiskIcon component (ShieldWarning/Shield/ShieldCheck), ToolIcon component, decision types 'allow_once'|'allow_always'|'deny' with scope 'session'|'permanent'

findings.description: Permission mode system exists with 4 modes controllable from status bar: Normal (ask for permission), Accept Edits, Plan (no execution), Bypass (auto-approve all)
StreamPermissionRequest and RiskLevel types defined in shared package
Cannot trigger a live permission dialog without sending a message that invokes a risky tool. The 'Normal' permission mode button is visible and clickable in the status bar.
